mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding aggregate income and consumption expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases occur in fewer than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% imposes a restrictive cost of capital that reduces equity risk appetite and compresses valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in 50% or more of months when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL with FEDFUNDS above 5% indicates that the real policy rate is insufficiently restrictive, requiring additional future tightening that the equity market must reprice lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term does not significantly improve predictive power over a linear sum of main effects (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is already low, a rise in UNRATE greater than 0.2 percentage points over three months marks the onset of labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during triggered late-cycle episodes, or positive returns occur in more than 50% of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in RSAFS below its 12-month trailing average signals collapsing consumer demand and impending inventory destocking, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls below its 12-month trailing average
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered months when RSAFS is below its 12-month trailing average over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate feeds directly into the cost of carry for equity futures and short-term discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month FEDFUNDS change and 5-day forward returns is positive over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in housing starts signal deteriorating construction employment and durable goods demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are not negative in more than 55% of instances following two consecutive monthly declines in HOUST over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive three-month change in industrial production reflects expanding physical output that translates into corporate revenue and earnings surprises, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following positive 3-month INDPRO momentum are not positive in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.
